description: Owen M. Fiss — The Forms of Justice (93 Harv. L. Rev. 1 (1979))
show_notes:
Owen M. Fiss — The Forms of Justice (93 Harv. L. Rev. 1 (1979))

Owen Fiss's 1979 Harvard foreword makes the affirmative case for structural, public-law adjudication. Against the view that courts exist merely to resolve private disputes, Fiss argues that their distinctive function is to give concrete meaning to public values — the task exemplified by institutional-reform litigation over schools, prisons, and mental hospitals — and defends the judge's expansive remedial role in reconstructing organizations to conform to the Constitution.

Chapters:
9:18 I. Adjudication and Public Values
37:51 II. Form and Function
1:43:11 III. The Problem of Remedy
